lines.gurmukhi: ਆਖੀਂ ਸੇਖਾ ਬੰਦਗੀ ਚਲਣੁ ਅਜੁ ਕਿ ਕਲਿ ॥੯੭॥
lines.translit: aakheen sekhaa bandagee chalan aj ki kal |97|
lines.english: O Shaykh, dedicate yourself to God; you will have to depart, today or tomorrow. ||97||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੋਰਾਂ ਸੇ ਨਿਮਾਣੀਆ ਬਹਸਨਿ ਰੂਹਾਂ ਮਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: goraan se nimaaneea bahasan roohaan mal |
lines.english: They remain there, in those unhonored graves.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਮਉਤੈ ਦਾ ਬੰਨਾ ਏਵੈ ਦਿਸੈ ਜਿਉ ਦਰੀਆਵੈ ਢਾਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa mautai daa banaa evai disai jiau dareeaavai dtaahaa |
lines.english: Fareed, the shore of death looks like the river-bank, being eroded away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਗੈ ਦੋਜਕੁ ਤਪਿਆ ਸੁਣੀਐ ਹੂਲ ਪਵੈ ਕਾਹਾਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: agai dojak tapiaa suneeai hool pavai kaahaahaa |
lines.english: Beyond is the burning hell, from which cries and shrieks are heard.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਮਲ ਜਿ ਕੀਤਿਆ ਦੁਨੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਸੇ ਦਰਗਹ ਓਗਾਹਾ ॥੯੮॥
lines.translit: amal ji keetiaa dunee vich se daragah ogaahaa |98|
lines.english: Those actions which are done in this world, shall be examined in the Court of the Lord. ||98||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨਾ ਨੋ ਸਭ ਸੋਝੀ ਆਈ ਇਕਿ ਫਿਰਦੇ ਵੇਪਰਵਾਹਾ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa no sabh sojhee aaee ik firade veparavaahaa |
lines.english: Some understand this completely, while others wander around carelessly.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਜ ਪਏ ਤਿਸੁ ਰਬ ਦੇ ਕੇਲਾਂ ਵਿਸਰੀਆਂ ॥
lines.translit: baaj pe tis rab de kelaan visareean |
lines.english: When the Hawk of God attacks, playful sport is forgotten.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦਰੀਆਵੈ ਕੰਨੑੈ ਬਗੁਲਾ ਬੈਠਾ ਕੇਲ ਕਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa dareeaavai kanai bagulaa baitthaa kel kare |
lines.english: Fareed, the crane perches on the river bank, playing joyfully.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੇਲ ਕਰੇਦੇ ਹੰਝ ਨੋ ਅਚਿੰਤੇ ਬਾਜ ਪਏ ॥
lines.translit: kel karede hanjh no achinte baaj pe |
lines.english: While it is playing, a hawk suddenly pounces on it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਮਨਿ ਚਿਤਿ ਨ ਚੇਤੇ ਸਨਿ ਸੋ ਗਾਲੀ ਰਬ ਕੀਆਂ ॥੯੯॥
lines.translit: jo man chit na chete san so gaalee rab keean |99|
lines.english: God does what is not expected or even considered. ||99||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਢੇ ਤ੍ਰੈ ਮਣ ਦੇਹੁਰੀ ਚਲੈ ਪਾਣੀ ਅੰਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: saadte trai man dehuree chalai paanee an |
lines.english: The body is nourished by water and grain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਲਕਲ ਮਉਤ ਜਾਂ ਆਵਸੀ ਸਭ ਦਰਵਾਜੇ ਭੰਨਿ ॥
lines.translit: malakal maut jaan aavasee sabh daravaaje bhan |
lines.english: But when the Messenger of Death comes, it breaks down all the doors.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਇਓ ਬੰਦਾ ਦੁਨੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਵਤਿ ਆਸੂਣੀ ਬੰਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: aaeo bandaa dunee vich vat aasoonee bana |
lines.english: The mortal comes into the world with high hopes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੇਖਹੁ ਬੰਦਾ ਚਲਿਆ ਚਹੁ ਜਣਿਆ ਦੈ ਕੰਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: vekhahu bandaa chaliaa chahu janiaa dai kana |
lines.english: Behold, the mortal being is going away, carried on the shoulders of four men.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨੑਾ ਪਿਆਰਿਆ ਭਾਈਆਂ ਅਗੈ ਦਿਤਾ ਬੰਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: tinaa piaariaa bhaaeean agai ditaa bana |
lines.english: It binds and gags the mortal, before the eyes of his beloved brothers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਅਮਲ ਜਿ ਕੀਤੇ ਦੁਨੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਦਰਗਹ ਆਏ ਕੰਮਿ ॥੧੦੦॥
lines.translit: fareedaa amal ji keete dunee vich daragah aae kam |100|
lines.english: Fareed, only those good deeds done in the world will be of any use in the Court of the Lord. ||100||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਹਉ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੀ ਤਿਨੑ ਪੰਖੀਆ ਜੰਗਲਿ ਜਿੰਨੑਾ ਵਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa hau balihaaree tina pankheea jangal jinaa vaas |
lines.english: Fareed, I am a sacrifice to those birds which live in the jungle.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਰੁਤਿ ਫਿਰੀ ਵਣੁ ਕੰਬਿਆ ਪਤ ਝੜੇ ਝੜਿ ਪਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa rut firee van kanbiaa pat jharre jharr paeh |
lines.english: Fareed, the seasons change, the woods shake and the leaves drop from the trees.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਕਰੁ ਚੁਗਨਿ ਥਲਿ ਵਸਨਿ ਰਬ ਨ ਛੋਡਨਿ ਪਾਸੁ ॥੧੦੧॥
lines.translit: kakar chugan thal vasan rab na chhoddan paas |101|
lines.english: They peck at the roots and live on the ground, but they do not leave the Lord's side. ||101||

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਾਰੇ ਕੁੰਡਾ ਢੂੰਢੀਆਂ ਰਹਣੁ ਕਿਥਾਊ ਨਾਹਿ ॥੧੦੨॥
lines.translit: chaare kunddaa dtoondteean rehan kithaaoo naeh |102|
lines.english: I have searched in the four directions, but I have not found any resting place anywhere. ||102||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਪਾੜਿ ਪਟੋਲਾ ਧਜ ਕਰੀ ਕੰਬਲੜੀ ਪਹਿਰੇਉ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa paarr pattolaa dhaj karee kanbalarree pahireo |
lines.english: Fareed, I have torn my clothes to tatters; now I wear only a rough blanket.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੑੀ ਵੇਸੀ ਸਹੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਸੇਈ ਵੇਸ ਕਰੇਉ ॥੧੦੩॥
lines.translit: jinaee vesee sahu milai seee ves kareo |103|
lines.english: I wear only those clothes which will lead me to meet my Lord. ||103||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇ ਪਟੋਲਾ ਪਾੜਤੀ ਕੰਬਲੜੀ ਪਹਿਰੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: kaae pattolaa paarratee kanbalarree pahiree |
lines.english: Why do you tear apart your fine clothes, and take to wearing a rough blanket?

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਘਰ ਹੀ ਬੈਠਿਆ ਸਹੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਜੇ ਨੀਅਤਿ ਰਾਸਿ ਕਰੇਇ ॥੧੦੪॥
lines.translit: naanak ghar hee baitthiaa sahu milai je neeat raas karee |104|
lines.english: O Nanak, even sitting in your own home, you can meet the Lord, if your mind is in the right place. ||104||

lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਗਰਬੁ ਜਿਨੑਾ ਵਡਿਆਈਆ ਧਨਿ ਜੋਬਨਿ ਆਗਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa garab jinaa vaddiaaeea dhan joban aagaah |
lines.english: Fareed, those who are very proud of their greatness, wealth and youth,

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਾਲੀ ਚਲੇ ਧਣੀ ਸਿਉ ਟਿਬੇ ਜਿਉ ਮੀਹਾਹੁ ॥੧੦੫॥
lines.translit: khaalee chale dhanee siau ttibe jiau meehaahu |105|
lines.english: shall return empty-handed from their Lord, like sandhills after the rain. ||105||

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਥੈ ਦੁਖ ਘਣੇਰਿਆ ਅਗੈ ਠਉਰ ਨ ਠਾਉ ॥੧੦੬॥
lines.translit: aithai dukh ghaneriaa agai tthaur na tthaau |106|
lines.english: They suffer terrible pain here, and hereafter they find no place of rest or refuge. ||106||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਤਿਨਾ ਮੁਖ ਡਰਾਵਣੇ ਜਿਨਾ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਓਨੁ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa tinaa mukh ddaraavane jinaa visaarion naau |
lines.english: Fareed, the faces of those who forget the Lord's Name are dreadful.

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਪਿਛਲ ਰਾਤਿ ਨ ਜਾਗਿਓਹਿ ਜੀਵਦੜੋ ਮੁਇਓਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa pichhal raat na jaagiohi jeevadarro mueohi |
lines.english: Fareed, if you do not awaken in the early hours before dawn, you are dead while yet alive.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਤੈ ਰਬੁ ਵਿਸਾਰਿਆ ਤ ਰਬਿ ਨ ਵਿਸਰਿਓਹਿ ॥੧੦੭॥
lines.translit: je tai rab visaariaa ta rab na visariohi |107|
lines.english: Although you have forgotten God, God has not forgotten you. ||107||

lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਲਹ ਸੇਤੀ ਰਤਿਆ ਏਹੁ ਸਚਾਵਾਂ ਸਾਜੁ ॥੧੦੮॥
lines.translit: alah setee ratiaa ehu sachaavaan saaj |108|
lines.english: To be imbued with the Lord God - this is the most beautiful decoration. ||108||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਕੰਤੁ ਰੰਗਾਵਲਾ ਵਡਾ ਵੇਮੁਹਤਾਜੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa kant rangaavalaa vaddaa vemuhataaj |
lines.english: Fareed, my Husband Lord is full of joy; He is Great and Self-sufficient.

lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦੁਖੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਇਕੁ ਕਰਿ ਦਿਲ ਤੇ ਲਾਹਿ ਵਿਕਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa dukh sukh ik kar dil te laeh vikaar |
lines.english: Fareed, look upon pleasure and pain as the same; eradicate corruption from your heart.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਲਹ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਭਲਾ ਤਾਂ ਲਭੀ ਦਰਬਾਰੁ ॥੧੦੯॥
lines.translit: alah bhaavai so bhalaa taan labhee darabaar |109|
lines.english: Whatever pleases the Lord God is good; understand this, and you will reach His Court. ||109||

lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦੁਨੀ ਵਜਾਈ ਵਜਦੀ ਤੂੰ ਭੀ ਵਜਹਿ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa dunee vajaaee vajadee toon bhee vajeh naal |
lines.english: Fareed, the world dances as it dances, and you dance with it as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋਈ ਜੀਉ ਨ ਵਜਦਾ ਜਿਸੁ ਅਲਹੁ ਕਰਦਾ ਸਾਰ ॥੧੧੦॥
lines.translit: soee jeeo na vajadaa jis alahu karadaa saar |110|
lines.english: That soul alone does not dance with it, who is under the care of the Lord God. ||110||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਰੀਦਾ ਦਿਲੁ ਰਤਾ ਇਸੁ ਦੁਨੀ ਸਿਉ ਦੁਨੀ ਨ ਕਿਤੈ ਕੰਮਿ ॥
lines.translit: fareedaa dil rataa is dunee siau dunee na kitai kam |
lines.english: Fareed, the heart is imbued with this world, but the world is of no use to it at all.

lines.translit: mahalaa 5 |
lines.english: Fifth Mehl:
